method_atom: A generic effective-action treatment of a tensionful extended object embedded in a curved, symmetric background: left to itself the object would collapse under its tension, but a topologically quantized flux threading its internal volume supplies a competing stabilizing energy, so the balance selects a discrete family of finite-size equilibrium configurations. From the effective action one derives the equilibrium sizes, energies, degeneracies/multiplicities, and the spectrum of small fluctuations, and shows these match an independent algebraic, symmetry-based prediction to all orders in a perturbative expansion parameter.
object_atom: D-branes wrapping group manifolds in string theory (concretely the SU(2) group manifold), whose worldvolume carries a quantized U(1) flux, described by the Born-Infeld action and characterized by their masses, RR charges, and agreement with boundary conformal field theory.
brief_reason: What transfers is the quantized-flux-balances-tension stabilization mechanism plus the effective-action derivation of a finite-size object's equilibrium spectrum matched to a symmetry prediction; what is domain-specific is the D-branes-on-SU(2)-group-manifolds string system with its Born-Infeld action, U(1) worldvolume flux and RR charges.
